hook: You already know how linear regression predicts a continuous outcome like salary or temperature — but what happens when your response variable is a yes/no decision, a count of events, or some other non-normal quantity? Forcing these situations into ordinary linear regression leads to nonsensical predictions, like probabilities greater than 1. This unit reveals the elegant framework that extends regression to handle almost any type of outcome imaginable.
concept: The unifying idea is that many different response types — binary, count, continuous — can be modeled through a flexible family of distributions linked to a linear predictor via a carefully chosen transformation, formally known as the 'generalized linear model with a link function.'

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to model binary outcomes by using the logit link to map a linear predictor onto probabilities, and to interpret odds ratios as meaningful measures of association.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Generalized Linear Models and Link Functions
topic_roadmap.description: Master the unifying GLM framework — exponential family distributions, link functions, and maximum likelihood estimation — that shows logistic regression and ordinary regression are special cases of one powerful structure.
Apply Poisson regression with the log link to model count data such as disease incidence or website visits, and learn to detect and handle overdispersion when the Poisson assumption falls short.
